article.description: — Jamal Murray scored 12 of his 26 points in the fourth quarter, and the Denver Nuggets rallied from 21 points down in the third quarter to beat the depleted Toronto Raptors 125-119 on Monday night.
article.headline: Jokic has 21st triple-double to rally Nuggets past depleted Raptors, 125-119

images.caption: Aaron Gordon spins for impressive and-1
images.name: Aaron Gordon spins for impressive and-1

article.linkText: Jokic has 21st triple-double to rally Nuggets past depleted Raptors, 125-119
article.story: DENVER -- — Jamal Murray scored 12 of his 26 points in the fourth quarter, and the Denver Nuggets rallied from 21 points down in the third quarter to beat the depleted Toronto Raptors 125-119 on Monday night. Murray added 12 assists to go along with Nikola Jokic’s 21st triple-double of the season. Jokic finished with 35 points, 17 rebounds and 12 assists to help Denver improve to 9-1 since the All-Star break. Jokic, who has 126 career triple-doubles, also had a season-high six steals, four in the third quarter when the Nuggets outscored Toronto 42-30. “He just does a really good job of anticipating and playing the angles,” Murray said. “It’s less about being in the spot, he’s playing the angle and the line of the pass. Shout out to Jok.” The Raptors played without Scottie Barnes (left hand fracture ), Chris Boucher (knee), Gary Trent Jr. (groin strain) and Immanuel Quickley (hip flexor) in addition to Jakob Poeltl being sidelined after having surgery on his left pinkie. That gave younger, less-used players a chance for meaningful minutes, including Jontay Porter, Michael Porter Jr.’s younger brother. It is the first time the siblings played in the same NBA game. “I was really happy for Jontay,” Porter Jr. said. “Not only him being out there but also the way he played. It was a special moment being out there together.” Jontay Porter scored a career-high 14 points, Kelly Olynyk scored 24 and Bruce Brown, who helped the Nuggets win the NBA title last season, had 13 points and 10 rebounds for Toronto The Raptors raced to a 22-point lead late in the second quarter and 68-51 at halftime. Denver rallied in the final minutes of the third to cut a 21-point deficit to 98-93. “An NBA game, you’re up 20 or down 20 the game is never over,” Toronto coach Darko Rajakovic said. “I know when they come out after halftime they were going to be more aggressive, and how to handle those moments is a learning opportunity for us.” The Nuggets trailed 107-104 with 7:59 left but scored eight straight points to take the lead for good. RJ Barrett, who had 26 points and nine assists for Toronto, cut it to 121-119 but Michael Porter Jr.’s corner 3-pointer with 1:02 left sealed it. “We knew our energy was a lot better than it was in the first half and that would get us back into the game,” Murray said. “I don’t know when we took the lead, I kind of zoned out. We did a good job of not playing the score, just playing hard.” The Raptors scored just 21 points in the fourth and missed all four of their 3-point attempts. “You can’t trade baskets when you’re down 20, so something’s got to improve,” Denver coach Michael Malone said. “We finally got defense back into the game.” UP NEXT Raptors: At Detroit on Wednesday night. Nuggets: At Miami on Wednesday night. ------ AP NBA: https://www.apnews.com/hub/NBA

articles.description: Jordan Clarkson's journey has shown Filipino players that they can belong on basketball's biggest stage, while Dylan Harper's emergence suggests they can also become stars there.
articles.headline: Why an NBA championship for Jordan Clarkson or Dylan Harper will mean everything to the Philippines

images.caption: Jordan Clarkson or Dylan Harper will become the first player of Filipino heritage to win the NBA Finals.
images.name: Jordan Clarkson and Dylan Harper [1280x720]

images.caption: Stephen A. Smith joins "SportsCenter" to weigh in on the 1999 Finals rematch between the Spurs and Knicks.
images.name: Why Stephen A. is so excited for Spurs-Knicks Finals rematch

articles.description: As Game 1 of the NBA Finals nears, New York Knicks guard Jalen Brunson, who has seen just about every defensive coverage the league has to offer, lauded San Antonio guard Stephon Castle on Tuesday, saying "he's going to be a great player, great defender."
articles.headline: Jalen Brunson wary of Stephon Castle's defensive prowess

articles.description: Gregg Popovich remains a trusted resource for the Spurs as they prepare for their first Finals without the legendary coach on the sideline.
articles.headline: Gregg Popovich a welcome resource for Finals-bound Spurs

images.caption: Wemby: Beating OKC is best preparation you can get for NBA Finals
images.name: Wemby: Beating OKC is best preparation you can get for NBA Finals

articles.description: The Knicks listed backup center Mitchell Robinson as questionable for Game 1 of the NBA Finals against the Spurs on Wednesday due to a broken finger.
articles.headline: Knicks' Mitchell Robinson (finger) questionable for Game 1
